entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The upper layer of earth comprising mineral particles, organic matter, water, and air, supporting plant life.

entities.name: WATER
entities.description: A transparent, odorless, tasteless liquid essential for most plant and animal life.

entities.name: PRODUCTION SYSTEMS
entities.description: The complete process of inputs, transformations, and outputs involved in producing agricultural commodities.

entities.name: WATERSHED
entities.description: An area of land where all water that falls in it drains to a common outlet.

entities.name: RAINFED AGRO-ECOSYSTEM
entities.description: An agricultural system relying primarily on rainfall for crop production.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The surroundings or conditions in which a person, animal, or plant lives or operates.

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Strategies for the sustainable use and protection of natural resources.

entities.name: SOIL QUALITY
entities.description: The condition of soil, including its physical, chemical, and biological characteristics, affecting its ability to support plant growth.

entities.name: WATER QUALITY
entities.description: The characteristics of water, including its physical, chemical, and biological properties, determining its suitability for various uses.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICAL REGIONS
entities.description: Geographical areas with moderate rainfall and warm temperatures.

entities.name: SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION PRACTICES
entities.description: Techniques used to prevent soil erosion and conserve water resources.

entities.name: PLANT NUTRITION
entities.description: The supply of essential elements required for plant growth and development.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The rate at which crops are produced.

entities.name: CATCHMENT
entities.description: An area of land where rainfall collects and drains into a common outlet, similar to a watershed.

entities.name: SURFACE WATER
entities.description: Water found in rivers, lakes, and streams.

entities.name: GROUNDWATER
entities.description: Water held underground in the soil or in pores and crevices in rock.

entities.name: CORRECTIVE MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Actions taken to address and improve issues identified through monitoring and assessment.
